name: Basic English Muffins
recipeIngredient: All-purpose flour for muffins, 4 cups divided
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together 2 cups of all-purpose flour, sugar, salt, and active dry yeast in a large bowl.
2. In another bowl, heat milk, water, and butter until very warm (120 to 130 degrees F). Gradually add this mixture to the flour mixture and beat at medium speed for 2 minutes. Add egg and 1 cup of all-purpose flour; beat at high speed for 2 more minutes. Stir in enough remaining flour to make a soft dough.
3. Knead on a lightly floured surface until smooth and elastic, adding more flour if needed. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled in size, about one hour. Punch down the dough and cover it again. Let it rise until doubled, about 45 minutes.
4. Divide the dough into two parts. On lightly floured surfaces, roll each part to be about 1/2 inch thick. Cut out muffins with a 3 1/4-inch round cutter. Place the muffins on lightly greased cookie sheets, about 1 inch apart. Sprinkle with additional cornmeal.
5. Cover and let rise until doubled again, about 45 minutes. Heat a lightly greased griddle or heavy skillet over very low heat.
6. Carefully remove muffins from dough using wide spatulas, being careful not to compress or puncture them. They should sound hollow when tapped. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes on each side until light brown.
7. Cool muffins on racks. Serve by splitting with fork tines and toasting.
